top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The US-Iran deal to end their war is reshaping the day: under a UN scheme, ships and some 11,000 stranded seafarers have begun moving through the Strait of Hormuz again, even as Washington and Tehran squabble over transit tolls and nuclear inspections. Europe is buckling under a record heatwave that triggered a major power outage in France and mass livestock deaths. In US politics, Congress passed a symbolic war-powers rebuke to President Trump over Iran and progressive candidates backed by Zohran Mamdani swept New York's Democratic primary, while a Big Tech-led sell-off dragged global markets lower.

stories.headline: Ships begin transiting Strait of Hormuz under UN evacuation scheme as US-Iran deal takes hold
stories.summary: Vessels have started passing through the Strait of Hormuz under a UN International Maritime Organization scheme to evacuate ships trapped by the conflict, an IMO spokesperson said on June 24. The plan, enabled by the US-Iran ceasefire framework, aims to move hundreds of ships and about 11,000 seafarers; ship-tracking data showed at least three vessels through and around 35 more preparing to sail. Washington and Tehran continue to spar over transit tolls, with President Trump saying Iran has told the US no tolls are being sought.

stories.headline: Record heatwave triggers major power outage in France as Europe wilts
stories.summary: A record-breaking heatwave across western Europe caused a major power outage in France affecting tens of thousands of homes and was blamed for mass poultry deaths, as temperatures hit record levels in several countries. Authorities across the continent rolled out cooling measures while the heat was forecast to peak mid-week.

stories.headline: US Congress passes war-powers rebuke to Trump over Iran strikes
stories.summary: Congress passed a war-powers measure rebuking President Trump over the Iran conflict, the first time it has done so, in a largely symbolic vote breaking with the president. The resolution asserts Congress's authority over military action but is expected to be vetoed.

stories.headline: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep New York Democratic primary
stories.summary: Candidates endorsed by Zohran Mamdani recorded a clean sweep in New York's Democratic primary, underscoring the growing influence of the city's progressive wing in intra-party contests.

stories.headline: Big Tech sell-off drags global markets lower; KOSPI and oil tumble
stories.summary: Sharp drops in Big Tech shares pulled Wall Street lower, and the rout extended into Asia, where South Korea's KOSPI tanked nearly 10% in the prior session before bouncing back. Oil traded near a four-month low as analysts flagged elevated volatility risks across equities and commodities.

stories.headline: Alibaba sues US government over Pentagon defence blacklist
stories.summary: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba has sued the US government over its designation on a Pentagon blacklist of companies alleged to have military ties, the latest legal pushback by a major Chinese firm against US national-security restrictions.

stories.headline: Anthropic's Mythos model found flaws in classified US systems, official says; China's 360 claims parity
stories.summary: A US official said Anthropic's Mythos AI model identified vulnerabilities in classified US government systems, an account reported by CNA. Separately, Chinese cybersecurity firm Qihoo 360 said it had developed tools to match Anthropic's Mythos, according to The Straits Times. Neither claim has been independently verified.

stories.headline: Airbus to inspect 16 A380s after cracks found on wings
stories.summary: Airbus has ordered inspections of 16 A380 superjumbos after cracks were found on the wings of an aircraft, a precautionary step affecting operators of the double-decker jet.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Security and housing led the local agenda: two self-radicalised Singaporeans, including a 19-year-old student, were issued ISA orders over Gaza-war-linked radicalisation, while the June BTO exercise drew strong first-timer interest in Prime and shorter-wait flats. Officials flagged a high risk of severe haze in the second half of 2026, and from July 1 SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore merge into a new statutory board. On the lighter side, Stray Kids announced a Singapore concert and a Teochew-dialect film sparked debate over local-language policy. Expect thundery showers today, 24-33C.

stories.headline: Two Singaporeans, including 19-year-old student, issued ISA orders over Gaza-war-linked radicalisation
stories.summary: Two self-radicalised Singaporeans, aged 19 and 30, have been dealt with under the Internal Security Act, the authorities said. The teenager subscribed to a 'salad bar' mix of extremist ideologies and was radicalised by the Israel-Hamas conflict; one of the men was assessed as willing to carry out attacks in Singapore if instructed by Hamas.

stories.headline: June BTO launch: strong first-timer interest in Prime projects and shorter-wait flats
stories.summary: HDB's June Build-To-Order exercise saw lower demand for shorter-waiting-time flats as more applicants flocked to Prime projects, with Woodlands flats and Prime developments in Bishan and Berlayar drawing first-timer interest, according to early application results.

stories.headline: High risk of severe haze for Singapore and South-east Asia in second half of 2026
stories.summary: A report warns of a high risk of severe transboundary haze in Singapore and the region in the second half of 2026, citing the developing El Nino, rising biofuel demand and Indonesian budget cuts to fire-prevention efforts.

stories.headline: New statutory board to merge SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore from July 1
stories.summary: A new statutory board will be set up on July 1, merging SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore to streamline the country's skills-training and employment-support functions, The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Jail for woman who scratched, slapped and punched her helper
stories.summary: A woman was jailed for repeatedly abusing her domestic helper over household chores, slapping, punching and scratching her and causing multiple injuries, a court heard.

stories.headline: NTUC lines up 150 jobs for some of 400 workers left unpaid by air-con firm
stories.summary: NTUC has found 150 job openings and is working to match all roughly 400 workers affected by unpaid salaries linked to an air-conditioning company, with affected workers also able to receive S$200 each in assistance, labour chief Ng Chee Meng said.

stories.headline: Stray Kids add Singapore stop to 'Run It' world tour
stories.summary: K-pop group Stray Kids announced a Singapore stop on their 'Run It' world tour in 2027 alongside a new album, 'This & That', drawing excitement from local fans.

stories.headline: Teochew film 'Dear You' sparks debate over Singapore's dialect rules
stories.summary: The Teochew-language film 'Dear You' has become a talking point, prompting commentary on whether it is time to rethink Singapore's long-standing limits on dialects in public media, with writers reflecting on older relatives who could not follow the dubbed dialogue.

stories.headline: Man who took free laptops via PayPal glitch reported himself, gets detention order
stories.summary: A 29-year-old Singaporean man who obtained free laptops by exploiting a PayPal glitch said he felt guilty and lodged a police report on himself; he was given a short detention order, Mothership reported.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran war is winding down: under the ceasefire framework signed on June 18, the UN has begun moving ships through the reopened Strait of Hormuz, the IAEA says inspectors will visit Iranian sites, and disputes now centre on transit tolls rather than fighting. In Lebanon, a fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire agreed June 19 has been repeatedly strained, with Israeli troops killing two in the south and Hezbollah demanding a timetabled withdrawal. On the Russia-Ukraine front, Kyiv knocked out power in Russian-occupied Sevastopol as it intensifies strikes on energy targets, while Moscow signalled it is waiting to gauge any shift in Trump's stance.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: War-ending deal holding; Hormuz reopening to shipping under UN scheme; toll and nuclear-inspection disputes unresolved

timeline.event: US and Iranian presidents sign a deal aiming to end the war.
First round of US-Iran implementation talks ends with what mediators call encouraging progress.
Dozens of ships head through the Strait of Hormuz after the deal; Iran says it has made no new commitments on nuclear sites after US Vice-President Vance said inspectors would be invited back.

Ships begin transiting Hormuz under a UN IMO scheme to evacuate hundreds of vessels and about 11,000 seafarers; the IAEA chief says inspectors will visit Iranian sites under the deal. Rubio rejects any Iranian transit tolls and Trump says Iran told the US no tolls are being sought, while Tehran calls the deal a 'declaration of US defeat'.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire (agreed June 19) repeatedly strained; sporadic deadly strikes in south Lebanon; new Lebanon-Israel talks opening

timeline.event: Israel and Hezbollah agree a ceasefire, the US says, after Lebanon reported Israeli strikes killing 47 and Israel said four soldiers were killed by Hezbollah.
Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including an Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say, as strikes continue despite the ceasefire.

Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after a lull, local authorities say; Hezbollah's chief demands a full, timetabled Israeli withdrawal and new Lebanon-Israel talks are announced. A UN commission of inquiry says Israel is committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children, a finding Israel rejects.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Ukraine intensifying strikes on energy targets in Russian-held areas; diplomacy stalled as Moscow waits to gauge Trump's stance

timeline.event: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russian-occupied Crimea knocks out power in Sevastopol, the peninsula's largest city, after striking its main substation; Kyiv has been intensifying attacks on energy facilities to dent Russian oil revenues and pressure Moscow to negotiate.

Russia says contacts with the US over Ukraine will resume when envoys Witkoff and Kushner are available, while Foreign Minister Lavrov says Moscow wants to know whether Trump's stance shifted after the G7.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is escalating rapidly and is now the dominant global health threat: WHO's latest outbreak news counted 915 confirmed cases and 234 deaths, while more recent reports cite over 1,000 infections and more than 260 deaths in DR Congo. On June 24, France confirmed its first-ever Ebola case - a doctor returning from DR Congo - the first confirmed case in Europe, though authorities put the public risk as very low. Conflict in eastern DR Congo is hampering the response. The earlier cruise-ship hantavirus cluster has had no new WHO update since late May.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) - DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating rapidly; conflict in eastern DRC hampering response; first case exported to Europe
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO DON608 (19 Jun), DRC + Uganda combined: DRC 896 cases / 232 deaths, Uganda 19 cases / 2 deaths; 88 recovered. France confirmed 1 imported case on 24 Jun (patient stable). More-recent reports (24 Jun, via France's health ministry and Africa CDC, cited by BBC) put DRC infections above 1,000 and deaths above 260 - not yet ratified in a WHO DON. 'countries' counts DRC, Uganda and France (1 imported case).

stories.headline: France confirms first Ebola case - a doctor returning from DR Congo
stories.summary: France confirmed its first-ever Ebola case on June 24 - a doctor who returned from a humanitarian mission in DR Congo, now in stable condition in a specialised facility. It is the first confirmed Ebola case in Europe; the health ministry said the risk to the population was 'very low' and was tracing the doctor's contacts. The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo species, for which there is no vaccine.

stories.headline: WHO reports rapid rise in DR Congo-Uganda Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: In its June 19 Disease Outbreak News, WHO reported 915 confirmed Bundibugyo-virus cases (896 in DR Congo, 19 in Uganda) and 234 deaths, with 220 additional cases and 96 deaths reported in DR Congo since June 13. Ituri province accounts for more than 90% of confirmed infections, and conflict involving the M23 rebel group is making the response harder. Africa CDC and US authorities warn it could become one of the largest Ebola outbreaks ever.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Actively exploited enterprise flaws and big breaches dominate this cycle. A high-severity Cisco Unified CM bug (CVE-2026-20230, CVSS 8.6) is now under attack after a PoC revealed a path to root, and the 'FortiBleed' campaign harvested credentials from internet-facing FortiGate firewalls at scale. Major data breaches piled up - Tata Electronics, healthtech firm Xolis (1.4 million people) and a LastPass exposure via the Klue supply-chain attack - alongside supply-chain poisoning of npm and WordPress plugins and a fake AI-agent 'skill' that slipped past security scans onto 26,000 agents. Law enforcement was busy too, with Scattered Spider guilty pleas over the TfL hack and a DoJ seizure tied to the Huione scam-laundering network.

stories.headline: Stealthy 'Mistic' backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
stories.summary: Researchers detailed a stealthy backdoor dubbed 'Mistic' tied to KongTuke, an access broker that sells initial network footholds to ransomware operators - making infections an early warning of a potential ransomware attack.

stories.headline: DoJ seizes Huione cloud account tied to cyber-scam money laundering
stories.summary: The US Department of Justice seized a cloud account linked to Huione, a network repeatedly tied to laundering proceeds from online scam and 'pig butchering' operations, the latest action against scam-economy infrastructure.

stories.headline: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
stories.summary: A high-severity flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager and its Session Management Edition (CVE-2026-20230, CVSS 8.6) is now being exploited after a published proof-of-concept revealed a file-write path leading to root, allowing an unauthenticated remote attacker to compromise affected systems. Cisco customers are urged to patch.

stories.headline: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
stories.summary: India's Tata Electronics confirmed a cyberattack after threat actors began leaking stolen data, with the attackers claiming to expose Apple and Tesla trade secrets. The company said it was investigating the scope of the breach.

stories.headline: Healthtech firm Xolis breach exposes data of 1.4 million people
stories.summary: Healthtech company Xolis disclosed a data breach affecting around 1.4 million people, exposing personal and potentially health-related information - a high-impact incident given the sensitivity of medical data.

stories.headline: New macOS 'ClickFix' attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
stories.summary: A new macOS ClickFix-style attack silently mounts disk images (DMGs) to deliver an infostealer, tricking users into running malicious commands and broadening the social-engineering technique beyond Windows.

stories.headline: 'FortiBleed' campaign harvested credentials from FortiGate firewalls at scale
stories.summary: The 'FortiBleed' campaign used a custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials from internet-facing firewalls in what reports described as a 110-million-credential harvesting operation; CISA has warned Fortinet customers, with tens of thousands of devices affected. Administrators are urged to rotate credentials and review exposed appliances.

stories.headline: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
stories.summary: Members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London, a notable accountability milestone against a prolific extortion-focused threat collective.

stories.headline: Fake AI-agent 'skill' passed security scans and reached 26,000 agents
stories.summary: Researchers reported that a malicious AI-agent 'skill' passed automated security scans and was reportedly installed on around 26,000 agents, highlighting the emerging supply-chain risk in agentic-AI ecosystems where add-ons run with broad permissions.

stories.headline: GitHub updates actions/checkout to block common pwn-request attacks
stories.summary: GitHub updated its widely used actions/checkout to block common 'pwn request' attack patterns in CI/CD pipelines, reducing a frequent path for attackers to abuse pull-request workflows in open-source projects.

stories.headline: LastPass confirms data breach via Klue supply-chain attack
stories.summary: LastPass confirmed a data breach stemming from the Klue supply-chain attack, in which abuse of OAuth tokens for the Klue app integration exposed customer data across multiple companies. Salesforce disabled the Klue integration in response.

stories.headline: Malicious npm packages pose as PostCSS tools to deliver Windows RAT
stories.summary: Attackers published malicious npm packages masquerading as PostCSS tooling to deliver a Windows remote-access trojan, the latest in a steady stream of open-source registry supply-chain poisoning targeting developers.

stories.headline: WhatsApp campaign uses fake business documents to install RMM tool
stories.summary: A phishing campaign abuses WhatsApp and fake business documents with VBScript to install the ManageEngine remote monitoring and management (RMM) tool, giving attackers hands-on control of victim PCs.

stories.headline: FFmpeg patches 'PixelSmash' flaw in widely used video decoder
stories.summary: FFmpeg fixed a flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' in a widely used video decoder; because FFmpeg is embedded in countless applications and services, downstream software that processes untrusted video may need to update.

stories.headline: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro plugins backdoored in supply-chain attack
stories.summary: Pro plugins from ShapedPlugin were backdoored in a supply-chain attack, potentially giving attackers access to WordPress sites that installed the trojanised versions. Affected site owners are urged to audit and update.

stories.headline: 29-year-old Squid proxy bug 'Squidbleed' can leak cleartext HTTP requests
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed 'Squidbleed', a 29-year-old bug in the Squid caching proxy that can leak cleartext HTTP requests, potentially exposing sensitive data passing through affected proxies.

stories.headline: 'DifyTap' flaws in Dify could expose AI chats across tenants
stories.summary: Researchers detailed 'DifyTap' flaws in the Dify AI application platform that could expose users' AI chats across different tenants, a multi-tenancy isolation failure with privacy implications for organisations building on the platform.

stories.headline: Canada's spy agency used first-of-its-kind warrant to clean botnet-infected devices
stories.summary: Canada's intelligence service used a first-of-its-kind warrant to remotely clean botnet-infected devices, a novel legal approach to disrupting malware infrastructure that raises both defensive promise and oversight questions.
